meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap by learning how to freelance pitch. Follow this beginner case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build real apps.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration. The fastest way to build a portfolio is to solve a real problem for a real person. By using freelance pitching to find clients, you move from 'tutorial hell' to 'shipping software.' Use Antigravity and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting of your first project.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic taunt. You’ve watched the tutorials, you know the syntax, but the 'project idea' well has run dry. This is the silent killer of every developer's career: the gap between knowing how to code and knowing what to build.

But here is the secret the senior engineers won't tell you: you don't need a groundbreaking idea. You need a client with a boring, solvable problem. By shifting your focus from 'creative genius' to 'technical problem solver,' you turn every pitch into a professional milestone. Let’s turn that blank screen into a production-ready application.

steps.title: Identify a Niche Local Problem
steps.description: Find a local business with a manual process that needs digitizing, such as a bakery needing an automated order tracker.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a technical consultant. Help me identify 5 common, manual workflows for a local coffee shop that could be solved with a simple SaaS dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze these 5 workflows and suggest a minimal viable feature set for each.'

steps.title: Craft the Value-First Pitch
steps.description: Write a pitch that focuses on the business outcome rather than the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a cold email to a business owner. Focus on saving them 5 hours a week of manual data entry. Keep it under 150 words and professional.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Draft a follow-up sequence for this pitch, emphasizing the security and speed of the proposed solution.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Architecture
steps.description: Once the client agrees, use AI tools to set up your project structure instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate project structure for a React/Node application. Include authentication and a database schema for tracking orders.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a new project with the provided boilerplate and install necessary dependencies.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on the specific features the client needs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a function that handles order status updates and sends a confirmation email to the client's customer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the order status update logic and unit test the edge cases.'

steps.title: Deploy and Iterate
steps.description: Ship the MVP and gather feedback to refine your skills.
steps.antigravity_prompt: What are the best practices for deploying a small-scale app to Vercel or Railway for a student project?
steps.claude_code_command: claude deploy 'Configure the production environment variables and run the build script.'

pro_tips: Always over-deliver on communication; 50% of freelance success is just being reliable and responsive.
Start with a 'Fixed Price' project to avoid scope creep while you're still learning.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; this makes your portfolio look professional.
Never build from scratch if a library exists; focus on the business logic, not the plumbing.
Treat your first client like your biggest client; their testimonial is worth more than the initial payment.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, record a 60-second Loom video demonstrating the app. Add this link to your LinkedIn profile and GitHub readme to start attracting your next client.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every successful engineer started with one small, messy project that they finished despite the doubt. You have the tools, the strategy, and the community to back you up. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
